steps.title: Interpret the constraint
steps.content: The phrase no more than 15 dollars describes a limit on spending. In inequality notation, no more than means $\le$. So the total cost must satisfy (total cost) $\le$ 15.

steps.title: Identify the cost expression
steps.content: Each pear costs 0.72 dollars, and we're buying $x$ pears, so the base cost is $0.72x$. The problem includes a 3-dollar adjustment (shown as a minus sign), making the total cost: $0.72x - 3$

steps.title: Set up and identify the inequality
steps.content:
Combining the cost expression with the constraint:
$$0.72x - 3 \le 15$$
That matches Choice C — $0.72x - 3 \le 15$.
